figures.gloss: The gruelling, multi-stage initiatory hermetic path designed to violently strip away the initiate's socially conditioned morality. It mandates dangerous physical trials, illegal acts, and the deliberate infiltration of extreme political groups to master temporal forces.
figures.facet: Initiation is not spiritual enlightenment but a deliberate traumatization and criminalization of the self to forge an amoral, predatory elite devoid of human empathy.
figures.provenance: Anton Long's 'Naos: A Practical Guide to Modern Magick'.

figures.gloss: Reconceptualized not as a hermaphroditic goat, but as a bloodthirsty, acausal Dark Goddess. She is the mother of the Dark Gods, demanding human sacrifice to manifest her horrific, anti-cosmic presence on Earth.
figures.facet: She severs the Great Mother archetype from fertility and nurture, recasting the divine feminine as an alien, predatory matriarch who feeds exclusively on human suffering.
figures.provenance: The 'Black Book of Satan' and internal O9A mythos.

figures.gloss: A prophesied, ruthless figure who will arise first as a terrestrial warlord to topple modern egalitarianism, democracy, and the 'Magian' (Judeo-Christian) ethos, forging a militaristic, neo-tribal imperium in their place. Only afterward, in the order's longer aeonic scheme, does this earthly conquest become the precondition for a later galactic-scale aeon of expansion — the texts compress the two stages, but Vindex himself is described as a terrestrial, not a spacefaring, figure.
figures.facet: A dark messiah whose immediate, singular function is the violent dismantling of universal human rights and liberal-democratic order on Earth — the 'galactic' aeon is a separate, later stage of O9A prophecy, not Vindex's own title or domain.
figures.provenance: Anton Long's 'Vindex: The Destiny of the West'.

figures.gloss: Lovecraftian, predatory entities dwelling in the acausal realm. They seek to breach the causal universe to consume human energy and dismantle the ordered structures of physical existence.
figures.facet: They are not fallen angels seeking redemption or rebels with a cause, but completely alien, entropic parasites that the initiate willingly invites in to accelerate cosmic collapse.
figures.provenance: O9A texts like 'Hostia' and 'The Grimoire of Baphomet'.

figures.gloss: The central O9A doctrine mandating human sacrifice—either through direct ritual murder or psychological manipulation leading to death—targeting individuals deemed 'dross' or enemies of the sinister dialectic.
figures.facet: Sacrifice is explicitly literal and sociopathic, stripped of any notion of cosmic renewal or propitiation, framed purely as a mechanism to harden the killer and unleash acausal terror.
figures.provenance: 'A Gift for the Prince: A Guide to Human Sacrifice'.

figures.gloss: The theory that human civilizations are living organisms with distinct lifespans that can be altered, corrupted, or destroyed by dark magic to disrupt the current status quo.
figures.facet: It instrumentalizes historical cycles. History is not observed or submitted to, but actively subverted by a tiny cabal using terrorism and occultism to force an artificial dark age.

figures.gloss: The cosmological map dividing existence into the ordered, physical universe (Causal) and an infinite, chaotic dimension of dark energy running backward in time (Acausal).
figures.facet: It rejects any synthesis or balance between the two realms, existing solely to teach initiates how to weaponize the chaotic Acausal to systematically destroy the Causal.
figures.provenance: 'Naos' and the fundamental physics framework of the O9A.

figures.gloss: The pseudonym behind virtually the entire O9A textual corpus — Naos, Hostia, 'A Gift for the Prince,' the Deofel Quartet novels — widely identified by researchers with David Myatt (b. 1950), a former British neo-Nazi organizer who later converted to Islam and still later described himself as a philosopher of 'pathei-mathos.' Myatt has repeatedly denied being Anton Long.
figures.facet: The order's entire mythos and seven-grade initiatory system rests on one contested authorial identity — whether Anton Long was Myatt alone, a Myatt-led circle, or a rotating collective pseudonym remains unresolved, which is itself consistent with the order's stated preference for anonymity over any cult of personality.
figures.provenance: Jacob Christiansen Senholt, 'The Sinister Tradition' (MA thesis, Aarhus University, 2009), which lays out the identification in detail; Connell Monette, 'Mysticism in the 21st Century' (2013); David Myatt's own public statements denying authorship.

figures.gloss: A gate or connection-point between the causal (ordinary physical) realm and the acausal realm of the Dark Gods — which can be a location, a rite, or a person, but which the order also uses as its own name for an autonomous small cell of practitioners, since O9A membership is organized as independent nexions rather than a central hierarchy.
figures.facet: It collapses cosmology and organizational chart into one word: by calling its own leaderless cells 'nexions,' the order makes its decentralized structure itself a piece of magical doctrine rather than a mere administrative choice.
figures.provenance: Anton Long, 'Naos: A Practical Guide to Modern Magick'; O9A organizational literature using 'nexion' for both site and cell.

figures.gloss: The foremost and most powerful of the Dark Gods in O9A mythos, his name glossed in the texts as 'an increasing of azoth' (an alchemical allusion). Presencing Atazoth into the causal world is presented as the aim of the order's highest-stakes, most advanced workings.
figures.facet: He personalizes what the collective 'Dark Gods' otherwise leaves abstract — naming a single chief lets the order frame its most extreme rites as service to one supreme, malevolent will rather than a diffuse cosmic principle.
figures.provenance: Anton Long, 'Naos'; O9A mythos texts discussing Atazoth as chief of the Dark Gods.

figures.gloss: An abstract multi-board game of pieces and transformations, played first as training in non-linear ('acausal') thinking and, at advanced grades of the Seven-Fold Way, worked as a form of aeonic sorcery in its own right.
figures.facet: It locates a working grade of sorcery in calculated strategy rather than in words, blood, or trance — the order's most distinctive claim that abstract game-play, pursued far enough, becomes magical technology.
figures.provenance: Anton Long, 'Naos: A Practical Guide to Modern Magick', which describes the Star Game at length.

figures.gloss: Mandated periods, typically six to eighteen months, of embedding within an extreme milieu — the military, organized crime, or a jihadist or neo-Nazi group — undertaken to destroy the initiate's inherited social conditioning and harvest lived experience the order's texts hold books cannot supply.
figures.facet: It converts real-world extremist affiliation into a graded magical exercise, which is precisely the mechanism researchers and security analysts cite when treating the O9A as an operational security concern rather than merely an esoteric curiosity.
figures.provenance: Anton Long, 'Naos'; Jacob Christiansen Senholt, 'The Sinister Tradition' (2009), discussing Insight Roles as documented O9A practice.

figures.gloss: A claimed rural English folk-tradition of wordless, empathic 'sorceresses' — non-literate, intuitive wise-women rather than ritual magicians — presented in O9A internal mythos as the muliebral (feminine) root of the order's 'sinister-numinous' current, in structural counterpoint to its predatory, masculine-coded Seven-Fold Way.
figures.facet: It supplies the order's own claim to internal balance: whether or not any Rounwytha lineage exists outside O9A's own literature, its narrative function is to hand an otherwise relentlessly predatory system a claimed empathic pole.
figures.provenance: O9A internal literature (material associated with the pseudonym 'Rachael Stirling'), treated as an unverified internal claim in Connell Monette's academic discussion of the order.

figures.gloss: 'Naos: A Practical Guide to Modern Magick' — the order's core operative manual, circulated in typescript from the 1980s, laying out the Seven-Fold Way's grade rituals and the Star Game; the order's most-copied and most-cited document.
figures.facet: It functions less as scripture to be believed than as a manual to be executed — its claimed authority rests on the grades and transformations it is said to produce in a practitioner's life, not on any revelation behind it.
figures.provenance: Anton Long, 'Naos: A Practical Guide to Modern Magick' (1980s typescript; multiple later editions).

figures.gloss: The order's long-game strategic doctrine: provoke overreaction, infiltrate institutions, and destabilize existing societal structures across decades, understood as the mechanism by which aeonic magick is supposed to act on history rather than on individual consciousness alone.
figures.facet: It is the hinge connecting occult theory to real-world political action — the doctrine that turns abstract 'aeonics' into a stated program of provocation and infiltration, which is why researchers treat the order's ties to political extremism as doctrinally motivated rather than incidental.
figures.provenance: Anton Long, 'Hostia'; Jacob Christiansen Senholt, 'The Sinister Tradition' (2009).

edges.note: Intra-tradition bond: Culling is the required physical mechanism to open the nexion (gate) that allows the Dark Gods to enter the causal realm.
Intra-tradition bond: Baphomet is the literal mother and sovereign commander of the pantheon of Dark Gods.
Intra-tradition bond: Vindex is the prophesied human catalyst who shatters the current Aeon and inaugurates the next cycle of civilization.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both involve harrowing, psychologically shattering journeys across distinct cosmic spheres, though O9A seeks sociopathy rather than community healing.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both are terrifying, blood-thirsty goddesses of war and destruction demanding absolute, bloody sacrifice.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Tezcatlipoca requires the blood of the captive to sustain the cosmos; O9A demands the blood of the 'dross' to feed acausal entities.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: While the Saoshyant arrives to perfect the world and end evil, Vindex arrives to violently destroy universalism and install a ruthless, unequal hierarchy.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Predatory, subterranean or acausal lords who actively scheme to trick, test, and consume human life.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The Acausal functions identically to the domain of Angra Mainyu—a realm of pure, destructive, and entropic force fundamentally hostile to the ordered cosmos.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: A terrifying, monstrous mother figure who embodies the absolute, devouring darkness of the cosmos.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both measure cosmic time in vast, structured epochs, but Aeonics seeks to forcefully abort the epoch rather than escape it.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The absolute ontological inversion of Jain non-violence; O9A posits that maximum, deliberate harm is the only path to spiritual evolution.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Hongik Ingan is the principle of benefiting all humanity; Vindex represents the absolute destruction of universal benefit in favor of a predator elite.

Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Etruscan discipline seeks to perfectly align with the will of the gods to maintain order; the Seven-Fold Way seeks to aggressively sever the initiate from all cosmic or social laws.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The Pleroma is the fullness of divine light beyond the material; the Acausal is the fullness of predatory, entropic dark energy beyond the material.
Dark-shard kinship: two unrelated systems that each built a doctrinal instrument for reclassifying murder as spiritual work — Aum by inverted compassion, the ONA by predatory selection.
Intra-tradition: author and core text — the order's practical manual is Anton Long's own composition.
Intra-tradition: Anton Long is credited as designer of the Seven-Fold Way's grade structure and rites.

Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: two real, contemporary founders of transgressive esoteric orders who wrote their own foundational grimoires under a chosen persona and built organizations around deliberately anti-establishment personal mythology — independent parallel careers, no claimed connection between them.
Intra-tradition: the nexion is the gate through which the Dark Gods are said to breach into the causal realm — already implicit in the roster's own 'opens the nexion' language.
Intra-tradition: Atazoth is named chief of the Dark Gods pantheon.
Intra-tradition: Baphomet is mother and commander of the Dark Gods; Atazoth their foremost chief — the two named heads of the same pantheon.

Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: both are named, chief entities whose ascendance is definitionally hostile to the ordered cosmos — though Atazoth is chief of a plural pantheon of Dark Gods, where Ahriman is Zoroastrianism's sole co-eternal principle of evil.
Intra-tradition: the Star Game is worked as an advanced-grade practice within the Seven-Fold Way's curriculum.
Intra-tradition: Naos is the text in which the Star Game is described at length.
Intra-tradition: Insight Roles are the mechanism behind the Seven-Fold Way's already-noted clause mandating 'deliberate infiltration of extreme political groups.'
Intra-tradition: the claimed empathic, wordless Rounwytha current stands as the order's own internal counter-pole to the predatory, ordeal-based Seven-Fold Way.
Intra-tradition: the Sinister Dialectic is the applied strategy; Aeonics is the underlying theory of civilizations as alterable living organisms.
Intra-tradition: culling is presented as one instrument in service of the broader provoke-and-destabilize strategy the Sinister Dialectic names.

Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: two unrelated systems that each built a doctrine justifying deliberate provocation of societal or apocalyptic collapse as a spiritual project — the ONA's decades-long infiltration strategy and Aum's accelerated Armageddon.
Same Lévi-derived hermaphroditic image, incompatibly read: chaos magic's planetary 'Earth Current' egregore against the O9A's bloodthirsty acausal Dark Goddess.

dossier.essence: A decentralized, self-described 'sinister' occult and Satanic movement that emerged in England in the 1970s–80s, its foundational corpus attributed to the pseudonymous 'Anton Long' (widely identified by scholars with David Myatt, an identification Myatt denies). It has no central authority — only a network of autonomous cells ('nexions') and a large body of texts — and it advocates an extreme, transgressive, elitist and neo-Nazi-adjacent path it calls the sinister tradition.
dossier.cosmos: Its framework divides existence into the 'causal' (ordinary space-time) and the 'acausal' (a timeless realm) and posits 'Dark Gods' as acausal entities to be channeled through 'nexions' (gates). History is read through 'Aeonics,' in which civilizations are organism-like 'aeons' the adept seeks to disrupt and drive toward a coming imperium. There is no conventional afterlife; the stated goal is the cultivation of enduring 'acausal' being.
dossier.practice: The core discipline is the solitary, graded 'Seven-Fold Way' (Neophyte to Immortal), involving magical workings, physical ordeals, and 'insight roles' that include deliberately infiltrating political extremes; the texts notoriously sanction 'culling' (human sacrifice) and antinomian criminality as tests of hardness. Practice is largely textual, secretive, and online, with rites such as the Star Game and the Ceremony of Recalling.
dossier.canon: A self-published corpus attributed to 'Anton Long' — 'Naos: A Practical Guide to Modern Magick,' the 'Black Book of Satan,' 'A Gift for the Prince,' the fictional 'Deofel Quartet,' and voluminous essays — circulated as samizdat and digital files rather than as any single authorized scripture.
dossier.reflects: It foregrounds religion as deliberate transgression — the sacred defined purely by the systematic violation of moral and social limits, and the initiatory ordeal inverted from healing toward the manufacture of a predatory self.
sources: Jacob C. Senholt, 'Secret Identities in the Sinister Tradition' (2013)
George Sieg, 'Angular Momentum: From Traditional to Progressive Satanism in the Order of Nine Angles' (2013)
Connell R. Monette, 'Mysticism in the 21st Century' (2013)
